name: Exam — Fluid Mechanics
description: Three-question exam covering fluid statics, pressure, buoyancy, and flow.

questions.text: Define pressure and state its SI unit. A container of fluid is placed in a zero-gravity environment. Describe the relationship between the pressure on opposing walls of the container and explain why no tangential force exists between the fluid and the confining surface in static equilibrium.

Explain the physical origin of buoyancy from first principles using pressure arguments. Derive an expression for the buoyant force on a fully submerged object of arbitrary shape and show that it equals the weight of fluid displaced.

State Bernoulli's equation and identify what each term represents physically. Using Bernoulli's equation, derive Torricelli's Rule for the exit speed of fluid through a small hole at the base of a large open tank of height H.
